title: Unclear error on call of non-static method using scope reference
body:
Hi! I'm not sure that this is a bug, but rather a lack of clear error message to facilitate debugging. It's quite non-obvious to resolve this first time, especially in large and complicated code.

Code where non-static method is called using scope reference mistakenly (or expected `static` qualifier is missing):

```verilog
module test;
  class foo;
    function void print();
      $display("I'm foo class");
    endfunction
  endclass
  
  class bar;
    function void hello();
      $display("I'm bar class");
      foo::print(); // <--- error
    endfunction
  endclass

  initial begin
    bar obj = new();
    obj.hello();
    $finish();
  end
endmodule
```

Verilator error:

```
In file included from Vtest__ALL.cpp:10:
Vtest_test__03a__03abar__Vclpkg__DepSet_hca57f31b__0.cpp: In member function ‘void Vtest_test__03a__03abar::__VnoInFunc_hello(Vtest__Syms*)’:
Vtest_test__03a__03abar__Vclpkg__DepSet_hca57f31b__0.cpp:13:11: error: ‘class Vtest_test__03a__03abar’ has no member named ‘__VnoInFunc_print’; did you mean ‘__VnoInFunc_hello’?
   13 |     this->__VnoInFunc_print(vlSymsp);
      |           ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |           __VnoInFunc_hello
```

At the same, this code in other simulators:
- "Error-[ISRF] Illegal scoped reference found"
- "A Non static class member cannot be accessed via the class scope resolution operator '::'."

This case can be quite frequent when non-stable code full of static/non-static methods is debugged.

Verilator version: Verilator 5.023 devel rev v5.020-171-gba47da658
OS: Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
